content:
Comprehension Debt
A May 2026 arXiv study found faster Copilot work without better comprehension, pushing inherited-system risk into review queues plus rollbacks.

6 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Copilot Can Speed Legacy Work Without Shipping the Codebase Map: A May 2026 arXiv study found faster Copilot work without better comprehension, pushing inherited-system risk into review queues plus rollbacks. Image created by the author with diffusion-synthesis and Python post-processing.GitHub’s green checkmark looks like a handoff receipt when the diff lands clean, but in an inherited revenue system it can hide the real debt. Approve enough AI-assisted PRs without owning the code path, and the work slips out of the editor and into review queues, rollback calls, incident notes, and the hours of the senior engineers who still know where the wires run.
That warning now has a clean proof object. In arXiv:2511.02922, last revised May 11, 2026, Yunhan Qiao, Md Istiak Hossain Shihab, Summit Haque, and Christopher Hundhausen studied 15 graduate CS students using GitHub Copilot on legacy-code feature work. Copilot improved speed and correctness; overall comprehension did not improve.
The map did not ship with the pull request.
For CTOs, VPs of engineering, staff engineers, and product operators rolling coding assistants into revenue-critical inherited systems, that split is the decision surface. AI can generate useful code; the question is whether your delivery…
